label: Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers

node.label: Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers
node.definition: A perceptron computes a weighted score and applies a threshold or activation, forming a minimal trainable neural classifier and historical basis for multilayer networks.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers is to connect Affine transforms & dense layers, Nonlinear activations to downstream capabilities including Tabular predictive analytics & decision support.
examples: substrate for Tabular predictive analytics & decision support
combined with Affine transforms & dense layers
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Affine transforms & dense layers, Nonlinear activations.

dependencies.rationale: Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Nonlinear activations.
Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers follows the learning setup expressed by Perceptron, Hebbian, Oja & spike-timing plasticity.
Perceptrons & single-layer neural classifiers follows the learning setup expressed by Supervised learning.
